track_characteristics.shape: Egg-shaped oval with an uphill run to the home turn and a sweeping home straight
track_characteristics.turn_bias: Fair track; spacious 400m home straight gives backmarkers and mid-fielders a genuine chance to run on late
track_characteristics.soil_type: Loam Turf (Slightly undulating terrain, handles rain well)

bias_notes.general: Unlike tighter regional tracks, Northam's long 400m home straight provides a fair playing field. Pace battles upfront can severely punish early speed, though leaders still do well on dry Good 4 tracks.

barrier_impact.sprints_upto_1100m: Inside barriers (1-5) are preferred due to the short run to the first sweeping bend.
barrier_impact.mile_1300m_1600m: Generous chute starts allow runners from wider barriers time to find cover before the main turn.

running_styles.leader: Moderate Advantage - Strong on Good tracks, but vulnerable if pressured early uphill
running_styles.on_pace: Strong Advantage - Ideal position entering the 400m straight
running_styles.midfield: Fair / Neutral - High success rate if trailing an active pace
running_styles.backmarker: Fair - Generous straight allows swoopers to make up ground late
